name: Shiva's Damaru
description: Shiva's Damaru is a small, two-headed drum that is deeply intertwined with the Hindu deity Shiva, the Destroyer and Transformer within the Trimurti. It is typically crafted from wood or metal, with leather stretched across both ends. Small beads or knots are attached to cords that strike the drumheads when the Damaru is rhythmically twirled, producing a distinct and captivating sound. The Damaru is not merely a musical instrument; it is a potent symbol representing the cosmic vibrations that create and sustain the universe. Its sound is believed to be the Nada Brahman, the primordial sound from which all creation emanates. Shiva's dance, the Tandava, is often accompanied by the rhythmic beat of the Damaru, signifying the cyclical nature of creation and destruction. The upper drumhead symbolizes the male principle (Shiva), while the lower represents the female principle (Shakti), emphasizing the balance and interdependence of these forces. The Damaru also represents the union of static and dynamic energies, illustrating Shiva's nature as both the still point and the source of all movement. It is considered a powerful tool for meditation, allowing practitioners to connect with the divine sound and achieve higher states of consciousness. The rhythmic beat is said to dispel negative energies and promote healing. The Damaru's association with Shiva makes it a revered object in Hindu temples and homes, often depicted in iconography and used in religious rituals. It embodies the divine power to create, preserve, and destroy, making it a central symbol in Shaivism.
subtitle: The Drum of Creation and Destruction
origin: Associated with Shiva since the beginning of creation; believed to have manifested with him.
currentLocation: Representations exist in temples and homes worldwide; the original is considered a divine attribute of Shiva.

keyMyths.title: The Sound of Creation
keyMyths.description: The Damaru's sound is believed to be the Nada Brahman, the primordial sound from which the universe was created. It represents the vibration that underlies all existence.

keyMyths.title: Shiva's Tandava
keyMyths.description: The Damaru accompanies Shiva's Tandava dance, a cosmic dance of destruction and creation. The drum's rhythm underscores the cyclical nature of the universe.

sources.author: Stella Kramrisch
sources.description: Explores the symbolism and iconography of Shiva, including the Damaru.
